---
format: html
title: get_spatial_model_params()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>get_spatial_model_params</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Get Spatial Model Fitting Parameters from Grid Catalogue</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Retrieves the model fitting parameters for a given spatial unit ID
from the project's spatial grid CSV catalogue.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>get_spatial_model_params(
  scale_id,
  file = here::here("Data/Input/spatial_grid.csv"),
  tax_res = "genus"
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="scale_id">scale_id</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single character string identifying the spatial unit.
Must match exactly one row in the catalogue file.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="file">file</code></td>
<td>
<p>Path to the spatial grid CSV file.
Default: 'here::here(&quot;Data/Input/spatial_grid.csv&quot;)'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="tax_res">tax_res</code></td>
<td>
<p>A single character string specifying the taxonomic resolution.
One of '&quot;genus&quot;' (default), '&quot;family&quot;', or '&quot;functional_type&quot;'.
Controls which set of model fitting parameter columns is used:
* '&quot;genus&quot;' — reads 'n_iter', 'n_step_size', 'n_sampling',
'n_early_stopping' (the original columns).
* '&quot;family&quot;' — reads 'n_iter_family', 'n_step_size_family',
'n_sampling_family', 'n_early_stopping_family'.
* '&quot;functional_type&quot;' — reads 'n_iter_ft', 'n_step_size_ft',
'n_sampling_ft', 'n_early_stopping_ft'.
'n_samples_anova' is always taken from the shared column
regardless of 'tax_res'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>Reads the CSV using 'readr::read_csv', filters to the row whose
'scale_id' column matches the supplied 'scale_id' argument, and
constructs the parameter list. Validation ensures the file is
readable, has a '.csv' extension, contains the required columns,
and that exactly one row matches the requested 'scale_id'.
'NA' values in the relevant 'n_step_size' and 'n_early_stopping'
columns are converted to 'NULL'.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A named list with five elements:
</p>

<dl>
<dt>'n_iter'</dt><dd><p>Integer. Number of training iterations.</p>
</dd>
<dt>'n_step_size'</dt><dd>
<p>Integer or 'NULL'. SGD mini-batch size.
'NULL' means auto (10 % of sites), corresponding to
an 'NA' value in the CSV.
</p>
</dd>
<dt>'n_sampling'</dt><dd>
<p>Integer. Monte Carlo samples per epoch.
</p>
</dd>
<dt>'n_samples_anova'</dt><dd>
<p>Integer. Monte Carlo samples for ANOVA
variation partitioning.
</p>
</dd>
<dt>'n_early_stopping'</dt><dd>
<p>Integer or 'NULL'. Early stopping patience — number of epochs
without loss improvement before training halts.
'NULL' means auto (20 % of 'iter'), corresponding to
an 'NA' value in the CSV.
Passed as the 'n_early_stopping' argument of
'fit_jsdm_model()'.
</p>
</dd>
</dl>



<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>get_spatial_window, get_active_config
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
